dataset: QBlade wind turbine simulation - Clark-Y vs NACA 4412 airfoil comparison

pi_items.source: UIUC Airfoil Data Site, Wikipedia Clark Y airfoil, Aerodesign.de historical records
PDAS NACA airfoil tables, UIUC Airfoil Data Site, NASA NACA airfoil documentation
NREL and academic research confirm TSR 6 is optimal at Reynolds 3×10⁵; research paper shows maximum Cp of 0.52 at TSR 6 for Re 300,000
Typical small wind turbine radius; consistent with research papers on small-scale HAWT blade design for 1-100kW range
NACA 4412 small wind turbine research papers document simulations at wind speeds of 2-10 m/s; 8 m/s is within typical range
U.S. Department of Energy Small Wind Guidebook: good wind turbines fall in 35-45% Cp range (0.35-0.45); verifier.py confirms same range
Research at Re 300,000 showed maximum Cp of 0.52 with NACA 4412 at TSR 6; 0.55 is upper conservative bound within Betz limit (0.593)
ResearchGate comparative study: 'NACA 4412 profile demonstrated superior aerodynamic stability with average efficiency 23% higher than Clark Y profile for small-diameter wind turbine applications'
Path format verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts; .wpa is QBlade project file format
Path format verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts; export_result.sh explicitly reads and analyzes this file

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and accurate. Clark-Y and NACA 4412 are well-documented real airfoils. NACA 4412 is confirmed superior for small wind turbines (23% higher efficiency in comparative studies). Design parameters are consistent with peer-reviewed research: TSR 6 and Reynolds 300,000 are optimal for this rotor scale; wind speed 8 m/s and Cp range 0.35-0.55 match standard wind turbine design values from DOE guidelines and academic literature. At these conditions, NACA 4412 achieves Cp ≈0.45-0.52, validating the expected range and winner declaration. File paths and project specifications are correctly formatted for QBlade.
